title: Queenstown vs Wanaka for Families — Which Should You Choose?
meta_desc: Compare Queenstown and Wanaka for family travel: activities, cost, and vibe
intro: When planning a family trip to the Southern Alps, the question on every parent's mind is: Queenstown or Wanaka? With both towns offering stunning scenery and adventure activities, the choice can be overwhelming. However, with prices starting from $150 per night for a family hotel room in Queenstown, it's essential to weigh up the costs and benefits of each destination.

sections.heading: Cost Comparison
sections.body: Accommodation prices are significantly higher in Queenstown, with an average family hotel room costing $200 per night, compared to $150 per night in Wanaka. However, Queenstown offers a wider range of accommodation options, including luxury resorts and budget-friendly motels.

sections.heading: Activities for Families
list: Skiing and snowboarding at Coronet Peak or The Remarkables ($120-$180 per day for a family lift pass)
Hiking the Ben Lomond Track ($0, free admission)
Visiting the Queenstown Gardens ($0, free admission)
Taking a scenic flight over Milford Sound ($200-$300 per person)
Renting bikes and riding the Queenstown Trail ($20-$50 per day)

sections.heading: Wanaka Activities for Families
list: Skiing and snowboarding at Cardrona or Treble Cone ($100-$150 per day for a family lift pass)
Visiting the National Transport and Toy Museum ($15 per person)
Taking a boat ride on Lake Wanaka ($50-$100 per person)
Hiking the Rob Roy Track ($0, free admission)
Renting bikes and riding the Wanaka Trail ($20-$50 per day)

sections.heading: Vibe and Atmosphere
sections.body: Queenstown has a lively, party atmosphere, with a wide range of bars, restaurants, and shops. Wanaka, on the other hand, is more laid-back and family-friendly, with a stronger focus on outdoor activities and community events. The drive between Queenstown and Wanaka takes around 1 hour, making it easy to visit both towns during your trip.

sections.heading: Suitability for Different Ages
sections.body: Both Queenstown and Wanaka are suitable for families with children of all ages. However, Queenstown may be more appealing to teenagers, with its range of adventure activities and nightlife. Wanaka, on the other hand, is ideal for younger children, with its more relaxed atmosphere and plenty of outdoor spaces to explore.

cta.text: Use our NZ Family Travel Budget Calculator

sections.heading: Getting Around
sections.body: If you plan to visit both Queenstown and Wanaka, consider renting a campervan or car. This will give you the flexibility to drive between the two towns and explore the surrounding countryside. You can find more information on campervan rentals on our campervans page.

cta.text: View our guide to family-friendly accommodation in Queenstown and Wanaka
